done_payload.summary: Implemented the lexer in `tinylang/lexer.py` which converts tinylang source code into a list of `Token` objects. The lexer handles whitespace, single-line comments, identifiers, keywords, numbers (integers and floats), double-quoted strings with escapes (\n, \t, \", \\), and both single and multi-character punctuation. It also tracks line and column numbers for each token and raises exceptions for unterminated strings or unrecognized characters.

All 11 tests passed. The lexer correctly handles numbers, strings (with escapes and error handling for unterminated strings), identifiers, keywords, punctuation (including multi-character ones), and comments. Line and column tracking are also correct.

The lexer implementation was already passing all 11 tests in the provided test suite. I verified the code by running `pytest` with `PYTHONPATH` set correctly, ensuring the `tinylang` module was importable. The lexer correctly handles whitespace, comments, numbers, strings (including escapes and multi-line error detection), identifiers, keywords, multi-character punctuation, single-character punctuation, and EOF. It also correctly tracks line and column numbers and raises exceptions for unrecognized characters with line/col information. All tests passed.
